title: Pan con Tomate
subtitle: Catalan tomato-rubbed grilled bread
excerpt: Pan con tomate is grilled bread rubbed with a cut tomato, garlic, and olive oil. It is a staple of Catalan and Spanish cooking, eaten daily as a breakfast, a snack, or the base of a tapa. The tomato must be very ripe and the bread properly toasted.
sourceNotes: Pa amb tomàquet (Catalan) or pan con tomate (Spanish) originated in Catalonia, where it is one of the most fundamental foods of daily life. The technique of rubbing a halved tomato directly onto toasted bread distributes the tomato juice, flesh, and seeds evenly across the surface.

content.text: Pan con tomate is the simplest expression of Spanish cooking: grilled bread rubbed vigorously with a ripe tomato until the flesh and juice soak into the surface, then finished with olive oil and salt. It works only with truly ripe tomatoes. Out of season, it is disappointing; in July or August, it is very good.

items.prepNote: or a similar rustic loaf, sliced thickly and on the diagonal

content.text: Toast the bread on a griddle pan or under a hot grill until well-marked and dry on the surface. The bread must be properly toasted, not just warm.
While the bread is still hot, rub the cut side of a halved garlic clove firmly over one surface of each slice. The warm bread grates the garlic slightly and leaves a light flavour.

Take a halved tomato and press it cut-side-down against the bread. Rub firmly and in circles, working the flesh into the surface. The bread should turn bright orange. Repeat until the tomato is exhausted, using a fresh tomato half for each two or three slices.
Drizzle {{extra-virgin-olive-oil}} of olive oil over the tomato-rubbed surface. Scatter sea salt flakes over the top. Serve immediately.

items.symptom: The tomato doesn't rub into the bread
items.cause: Bread too soft or not toasted enough
items.fix: The bread must be properly crisp and dry before the tomato goes on. A soft interior is fine; the crust and surface need to be dry enough to absorb the tomato juice.

items.cause: Tomato was not ripe or not enough salt and oil
items.fix: Pan con tomate is only as good as the tomato. Use the ripest, most fragrant tomato you have. Be generous with the olive oil and salt.

content.text: Pa amb tomàquet is the daily bread of Catalonia. Catalans eat it for breakfast, as a snack, and as the base for almost every tapa. A piece of bread, a ripe tomato, and good olive oil is breakfast in many Catalan households. It is also used as the base for cured meats and cheese at the table. Outside Catalonia it is sometimes served pre-rubbed, but the cut tomato at the table and the act of rubbing is part of the ritual.
